dataAvailability.executive: Xi Jinping holds supreme authority as General Secretary of the CCP and CMC Chairman; Li Qiang is Premier (head of government).

metadata.officialName: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

dataAvailability.executive: CDU/CSU won the Feb 2025 election; Merz became Chancellor in April 2025.

dataAvailability.executive: UAE is a federal monarchy; no elections for executive positions.
King Salman is head of state; his son Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (MBS) serves as Prime Minister and is de facto ruler since 2022.
dataAvailability.elections.legislative: No legislative election data available for 'SA'.

dataAvailability.executive: Mahmoud Abbas has ruled by decree since Palestinian elections were last held in 2006. PM Mohammad Mustafa appointed March 2024.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: World Bank data for Palestine is limited due to its political status.

dataAvailability.executive: Mexico has a presidential system; Sheinbaum is both head of state and head of government. First elected female president of Mexico.

headOfState.name: President – Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva

headOfGovernment.name: President – Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva

executiveInPower.leader: President – Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva

dataAvailability.executive: Mark Carney became PM on 14 March 2025 after Trudeau's resignation. Liberals won the 28 April 2025 election with 169 seats — a minority government, 3 seats short of majority. Several opposition MPs have since crossed the floor.
Sanae Takaichi became Japan's first female PM in October 2025. She called a snap election on 8 February 2026, winning a historic LDP supermajority (316/465 seats in the lower house).

Ali Khamenei was killed on 28 Feb 2026 in US/Israeli strikes. A three-member interim council (President, Chief Justice, Guardian Council rep) is overseeing transition to elect a new Supreme Leader. Masoud Pezeshkian remains President.
Assad fled in December 2024. Ahmad al-Sharaa (formerly Abu Mohammad al-Jolani) leads the transitional government. No functioning legislature.
dataAvailability.elections.legislative: No legislative election data available for 'SY'.

bodies.name: National Assembly
bodies.inControl: No single majority (hung parliament; left-wing NFP largest bloc)

dataAvailability.executive: François Bayrou became PM in January 2025 after Michel Barnier's government fell in December 2024.
Turkey has a presidential system; Erdoğan is both head of state and government.

Nicolás Maduro fled Venezuela in late 2025; Vice President Delcy Rodríguez became acting president on 3 January 2026. Edmundo González is internationally recognised by some states as legitimate president-elect.

dataAvailability.executive: Lương Cường became President in October 2024. The General Secretary (Tô Lâm) holds supreme authority as party leader.

bodies.name: Legislative Yuan
bodies.inControl: Kuomintang (KMT) and Taiwan People's Party (TPP) hold combined majority

dataAvailability.executive: Taiwan is not a UN member state; its sovereignty is disputed. Lai Ching-te (William Lai) became president in May 2024.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Taiwan is not a UN member state and is not recognised by the World Bank. WGI data is unavailable.
dataAvailability.elections.legislative: Taiwan is not a member of the IPU and is absent from the Parline database.

dataAvailability.executive: Lee Jae-myung won the June 2025 snap presidential election following Yoon Suk-yeol's impeachment and removal for his December 2024 martial law attempt.
Kim Jong-un holds supreme authority as General Secretary of the KWP, President of State Affairs, and Supreme Commander.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: North Korea governance data is based on limited external assessments.
dataAvailability.elections.legislative: North Korea holds nominal single-party elections; meaningful data unavailable.

dataAvailability.executive: Prabowo Subianto became president in October 2024, succeeding Joko Widodo.

metadata.officialName: Republic of the Union of Myanmar

dataAvailability.executive: Myanmar has been under military junta (SAC) rule since the February 2021 coup. The elected NLD government operates in exile as the National Unity Government.
Somalia has a fragile federal government; direct elections are limited. Hassan Sheikh Mohamud was re-elected by parliament in May 2022.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Somalia governance data is based on limited external assessments.

dataAvailability.executive: Yemen is split between the Houthi-controlled north (Ansar Allah) and the internationally recognised government in the south. The Presidential Leadership Council (est. 2022) chairs the recognised government.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Yemen governance data reflects the internationally recognised government baseline; current effective governance is severely disrupted by civil war.

dataAvailability.executive: Libya has two rival governments: the UN-recognised Government of National Unity (GNU) in Tripoli, and the rival government backed by the House of Representatives in Benghazi/Tobruk.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Libya has parallel governing authorities; data reflects the internationally recognised Government of National Unity's institutional capacity.

dataAvailability.executive: Dina Boluarte became president in December 2022 after Pedro Castillo's impeachment. She has governed without a stable congressional majority.

dataAvailability.executive: King Frederik X succeeded Queen Margrethe II on 14 January 2024. PM Frederiksen called a snap election for 24 March 2026, capitalising on popularity from her stance against Trump's Greenland threats.
Sudan has been in civil war since April 2023 between the SAF (al-Burhan) and RSF (Hemeti). The civilian transitional framework has collapsed.
dataAvailability.worldBankGovernance: Sudan is in active civil war (SAF vs RSF since April 2023); governance data is from pre-war assessments and does not reflect current conditions.

dataAvailability.executive: Elections are suspended under martial law due to the ongoing Russian invasion. Zelensky's term was extended per wartime provisions.
